name: Agworld Crop Management & Farm Advisory API
what_it_does: Agworld collaborative crop management and farm advisory platform REST API for grower-agronomist collaboration, crop planning, chemical application records, and compliance reporting. Enables AI agents to manage farm field and paddock boundary management for digital farm map automation, handle crop plan and agronomic recommendation creation for precision farming advice automation, access chemical and fertilizer application record-keeping for farm compliance automation, retrieve grower-agronomist collaboration workflow for ag retail advisory automation, manage crop monitoring and growth stage tracking for agronomic decision automation, handle spray diary and chemical use recording for regulatory compliance automation, access soil test and nutrient recommendation integration for precision soil management, retrieve yield and harvest data for crop performance analysis automation, manage chemical inventory and purchase order tracking for farm input management, and integrate Agworld with Trimble Agriculture, precision ag hardware, and ag chemical supply chain platforms for full farm management ecosystem.
use_cases: Creating crop plans from agronomic advisory agents
Recording chemical applications from compliance agents
Tracking growth stages from crop monitoring agents
Managing grower-agronomist collaboration from advisory agents
not_for: Machine telemetry and equipment fleet management (use John Deere OC or Trimble Agriculture)
best_when: An agronomist, ag retailer, or farming operation (particularly in Australia, US, or Canada) wanting AI agents to automate crop planning, chemical application records, grower-agronomist collaboration, and farm compliance reporting within Agworld.
avoid_when: CHEMICAL APPLICATION REGULATORY RECORD ACCURACY FOR COMPLIANCE: Automated chemical application records in Agworld for regulatory compliance (Australian APVMA, US EPA, export market MRL compliance) must record correct product, rate, timing, and withholding period; automated record with incorrect application details creates produce residue compliance failure and export market rejection risk. WITHHOLDING PERIOD ENFORCEMENT BEFORE HARVEST SCHEDULING: Automated harvest scheduling must check chemical application withholding periods for all products applied to the crop; automated harvest before withholding period expiry for registered chemicals creates produce food safety violation and potential market recall. GROWER CONSENT FOR DATA SHARING WITH THIRD PARTIES: Agworld grower farm data (field boundaries, crop plans, chemical records) is owned by the grower; automated sharing of grower farm data with third parties (input suppliers, lenders, insurers) via Agworld API requires explicit grower consent; unauthorized data sharing creates privacy violation and trust relationship damage in ag retail context.

auth.notes: Agworld uses OAuth 2.0 with scopes. REST API with JSON. Perth, Western Australia HQ (Agworld Pty Ltd). Founded 2010 by David McKeon. Private. Australian crop management and agronomic advisory platform. Strong Australian grains, horticulture, and broadacre agriculture presence. US expansion (California, Pacific Northwest specialty crops). Grower-agronomist collaboration model. Chemical use compliance focus. Competes with Trimble Precision-IQ, Agrivi, and Cropio for collaborative farm management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free grower account with basic farm management; Agronomist paid subscription for advisory and compliance features
pricing.paid_tiers: Grower: free basic; Agronomist: annual subscription per seat; Agribusiness: enterprise pricing for ag retail organizations
pricing.notes: Perth WA Australia. Private. Founded 2010. Australian and US specialty crop focus. Freemium grower account. Annual subscription for agronomist and agribusiness tiers.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; farm data and application record queries throttled; no webhooks — polling required

known_agent_gotchas: NO WEBHOOKS — FARM DATA EVENTS REQUIRE POLLING: Agworld does not support native webhooks; automated crop plan updates, chemical application records, and grower-agronomist activity require scheduled polling; design polling intervals aligned with farming seasonal cadence (planting, crop monitoring, application, harvest phases)

WITHHOLDING PERIOD DATABASE ACCURACY FOR HARVEST SCHEDULING: Automated harvest scheduling must reference current registered chemical withholding periods from APVMA (Australia) or EPA (US) database; Agworld chemical database may not reflect real-time registration changes; automated harvest scheduling using Agworld withholding data must be validated against current regulatory registration

GROWER-AGRONOMIST AUTHORIZATION MODEL FOR DATA ACCESS: Agworld implements grower-agronomist authorization where agronomist access to grower farm data requires grower invitation and acceptance; automated workflows operating on behalf of agronomist must correctly authenticate as agronomist and operate only on grower farms with active agronomist authorization; automated data access across unauthorized grower farms creates privacy violation

Australian APVMA regulatory vs US EPA registration cross-market: Agworld operates in both Australia and US; chemical products have different registration status and label requirements in each jurisdiction; automated chemical application record creation must apply jurisdiction-specific product registration; Australian APVMA-only registered product applied in US context creates FIFRA violation

Export market maximum residue limit (MRL) compliance for specialty crops: Automated Agworld chemical application records for export crops (Australian wheat, US specialty produce) must track residues against destination country MRL requirements; Australian wheat export to Japan, EU, or South Korea has different MRL requirements than domestic; automated application record without export market MRL check creates export produce rejection risk

Prescription integration with precision ag hardware — Agworld crop plan prescriptions may need delivery to field equipment via Trimble Agriculture or John Deere Operations Center integration; Agworld-to-machine-controller workflow requires separate FMIS integration bridge; automated Agworld prescription creation does not directly update machine controller without third-party FMIS integration
agent_readiness.security_notes: Farm advisory and crop management. SOC2. OAuth2. AU/US. Farm boundary, crop plan, and chemical application data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Crop management. SOC2. OAuth2. AU/US. Grower farm data and chemical application records.
